## reference · Consistency and Standards Heuristic
* Maintain **internal consistency** within a product or family of products by using the same patterns everywhere inside the system.
* Follow **external consistency** by adhering to web-, platform-, and domain-specific conventions.
* Use **established conventions** in an industry or on the web at large to make interactions consistent with what people expect.
* Ensure **visual consistency** by using consistent symbols, iconography, and imagery across the site.
* Maintain **page and button layout consistency** by reusing elements such as headings, call-to-action buttons, and navigation across pages.
* Ensure **user-entered data consistency** by using industry standards for data input, such as date pickers and phone number formatting.
* Create a **design system** to help teams maintain consistency across products and services.
* Follow **Jakob's Law**, which states that people spend most of their time on sites other than yours, and bring their expectations from other sites when they visit your site.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/consistency-and-standards/
